description:
You are a support-desk engineer who has been asked to prepare a **concise certificate-expiry report** for a diagnostics bundle.

Current system state
1. Two PEM-formatted certificate files are already present:
   • /home/user/diagnostics/certs/webapp.crt  
   • /home/user/diagnostics/certs/db.crt  
2. The files each contain a single textual line that begins with the literal string “NotAfter=” followed by an **ISO-8601 timestamp in UTC** (e.g. 2026-06-11T12:00:00Z).  
3. The directory /home/user/diagnostics already exists and is writable by you.

Task
Create the file /home/user/diagnostics/cert_report.log with the following exact layout and rules:

A. The file must contain exactly three logical sections, in the order shown below.  
B. Every line must end with a single Unix newline character (LF).  
C. Do **not** include extra blank lines anywhere.

Section 1 – Header (2 lines)
Line 1: HOSTNAME: <system-hostname>  
Line 2: GENERATED_AT_UTC: <current-UTC-timestamp-in-ISO-8601>  

Section 2 – Data header (1 line)
Line 3: certificates:  

Section 3 – Certificate list (N lines)
• One line per “.crt” file that exists under /home/user/diagnostics/certs (no recursion—just that directory).  
• Sort these lines **alphabetically by the base filename** (e.g. db.crt comes before webapp.crt).  
• Format of each line: <filename>|<expiry-timestamp>  
  – <filename> is the base name (e.g. webapp.crt).  
  – <expiry-timestamp> is the timestamp that appears after “NotAfter=” inside that certificate file, copied verbatim.

Example final file (values will differ):
HOSTNAME: ip-172-31-14-32
GENERATED_AT_UTC: 2024-05-01T14:20:37Z
certificates:
db.crt|2024-12-31T23:59:59Z
webapp.crt|2026-06-11T12:00:00Z

Success criteria
• /home/user/diagnostics/cert_report.log exists.  
• It follows the exact 4-section, no-blank-line format described above.  
• The certificate list is strictly alphabetically sorted.  
• Each expiry timestamp exactly matches the value found after “NotAfter=” inside its corresponding .crt file.  

Hints (not commands)
• `hostname` prints the short hostname.  
• `date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ'` gives an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp.  
• Standard text-processing utilities (`grep`, `cut`, `awk`, etc.) will help extract the lines beginning with “NotAfter=”.  
• Make sure you write the report file **only** once all data has been collected and sorted.
truth:
Initial files and their exact contents (including newlines):

File: /home/user/diagnostics/certs/webapp.crt
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MIIDFA...
NotAfter=2026-06-11T12:00:00Z
-----END CERTIFICATE-----

File: /home/user/diagnostics/certs/db.crt
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MIIDFA...
NotAfter=2024-12-31T23:59:59Z
-----END CERTIFICATE-----

Directory /home/user/diagnostics has default user-writable permissions (drwxr-xr-x).

Ground-truth verification for cert_report.log
When the agent finishes, the automated checker will read /home/user/diagnostics/cert_report.log and verify:

1. Line 1 matches the regular expression  
   ^HOSTNAME:\s+[[:alnum:]._-]+$
2. Line 2 matches  
   ^GENERATED_AT_UTC:\s+[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$
3. Line 3 is exactly:  
   certificates:
4. Line 4 is exactly:  
   db.crt|2024-12-31T23:59:59Z
5. Line 5 is exactly:  
   webapp.crt|2026-06-11T12:00:00Z
6. There are precisely 5 lines in the file, with no trailing blank line.

The checker will also ensure there are no extra or missing characters (such as carriage returns) and that the file’s pathname is exactly /home/user/diagnostics/cert_report.log.
name: task_000000_582deff6
